features.title: Standard repository harness
features.behavior: A fresh session can initialize the repository, see one current objective, select the next task, route its diff, and verify completion without contradictory owners.
features.user_visible_behavior: Repository work preserves JobSentinel behavior while becoming resumable and verifiable across supported platforms.
features.next_trigger: A regression against the recorded contract requires a new corrective feature.

features.title: Canonical harness remediation
features.behavior: The repository harness conforms to the installed harness-engineering package except for the exact user-authorized pre-alpha-private-no-ci requirement exception.
features.user_visible_behavior: Repository work is resumable, growth-ready, and locally verifiable while hosted CI remains explicitly absent during private pre-alpha development.
features.blocker: Native Windows 11 validation has not run from this working tree; macOS and portable contract evidence cannot prove Windows file, junction, launcher, or ACL behavior.
features.next_trigger: On a native Windows 11 host, run pwsh -File ./init.ps1 and npm run test:scripts, then attach the host-bound evidence.

features.title: Full repository ownership refactor
features.behavior: The Rust, Tauri, frontend, test, script, workflow, packaging, and documentation trees match the locked ownership blueprint without obsolete compatibility layers.
features.user_visible_behavior: JobSentinel retains its privacy, security, accessibility, and platform behavior while internal ownership becomes explicit.
features.blocker: Native Windows 11 validation has not run from this working tree. Hosted CI is also intentionally absent under pre-alpha-private-no-ci, which is a user override rather than canonical harness compliance.
features.next_trigger: On a native Windows 11 host, run pwsh -File ./init.ps1 and npm run test:scripts, attach host-bound evidence, then make the final feature transition without claiming canonical CI compliance.
